tools.name: OCR
tools.description: This tool can recognize all text on the input image.

outputs.description: OCR results, include bbox in x1, y1, x2, y2 format and the recognized text.

tools.name: ImageDescription
tools.description: A useful tool that returns a brief description of the input image.

tools.name: RegionAttributeDescription
tools.description: Describe the attribute of a region of the input image.

inputs.name: bbox
inputs.description: The bbox coordinate in the format of `(x1, y1, x2, y2)`

dialogs.content: What type of MRI is used in this image? Please infer from any labels/axes and the figure content.

dialogs.thought: Check axes/labels to determine whether the figure represents a dynamic MRI measurement (e.g., signal vs time).
Understand whether the figure represents a time-dependent contrast uptake curve typical of dynamic MRI.

content.content: A line graph plotting signal enhancement versus time (minutes), with multiple curves (circles, squares, triangles) and error bars, consistent with a dynamic contrast enhancement time-course plot rather than an anatomic MRI slice.

arguments.attribute: what is being plotted and what it implies about MRI acquisition technique

dialogs.thought: Confirm that signal enhancement over time indicates contrast kinetics, characteristic of dynamic contrast-enhanced MRI.

content.content: The plot shows signal enhancement over time following contrast administration, with multiple curves indicating different conditions, consistent with dynamic contrast uptake and washout measurement typical of DCE-MRI.

dialogs.content: The image is based on dynamic contrast-enhanced MRI using a macromolecular contrast agent called BSA-Gd-DTPA. This type of MRI allows for the visualization of time-dependent changes in signal enhancement in tumors.
